description: Patch: Codex sub-agent collab-tool hard disable (#240/#241), Windows Python detection fallback (#236), Codex agent template drift fix.
changelog:
**Bug Fixes:**
- fix(codex): disable Codex collab tools in sub-agent role files at protocol level (#240, #241). 0.5.5 removed the SessionStart injection vector, 0.5.6 added prompt-layer `fork_turns="none"` guidance ‚Äî but [Ca11back's reproduction on #241](https://github.com/mindfold-ai/Trellis/issues/241) showed the main agent still spawned with default `fork_turns="all"`, the child inherited the parent's `spawn_agent(...)` records, applied the "wait for spawned subagents" rule to itself, and `wait_agent`-stuck on phantom children. Structural fix: each `codex/agents/trellis-{implement,check,research}.toml` now sets `[features].multi_agent = false` and `[features.multi_agent_v2].enabled = false`. Codex's `tool_config.rs:179-180` gates `spawn_agent` / `wait_agent` / `list_agents` / `close_agent` registration on both flags being on; with both off the tools don't exist in the sub-agent's tool list ‚Äî the model literally cannot call them. Codex agent role files support full ConfigToml override layers (`codex-rs/core/src/config/agent_roles.rs:217-225`), `[features]` included. Adds `[issue-241-followup]` regression test asserting all three template toml files contain the disable block.
- fix(codex): port "Load Trellis Context First" section back to template (drift). The Codex sub-agent role files in `.codex/agents/` (project dogfood) had a 27-line section telling the sub-agent how to find its active task path on class-2 platforms (look at the dispatch prompt's `Active task: <path>` first line, fall back to `task.py current --source`, then ask the user) ‚Äî but the canonical template files in `packages/cli/src/templates/codex/agents/trellis-{implement,check}.toml` were missing it. Drift went undetected: dogfood was richer than what `trellis init` shipped to user projects. Fix: copy the section into both template files. `trellis-research.toml` was already in sync.
- fix(init): Windows Python detection fallback chain (#236). `trellis init` previously tried only `python --version` on Windows. If the host had Python under `python3` (Microsoft Store) or `py -3` (python.org launcher) but not `python`, init failed outright with `Python command "python" not found`. Workaround was to install 0.4.0 first then upgrade. Now `resolveSupportedPython()` walks `python ‚Üí python3 ‚Üí py -3` on Windows (`python3 ‚Üí python` elsewhere) and uses the first candidate that probes Python ‚â• 3.9. The resolved command is cached via `setResolvedPythonCommand()` in `configurators/shared.ts` so `replacePythonCommandLiterals()` and all downstream template / configurator writes pick up the same value. Adds two env-var escape hatches: `TRELLIS_PYTHON_CMD=<cmd>` (explicit override, no probe) and `TRELLIS_SKIP_PYTHON_CHECK=1` (skip probe entirely ‚Äî pre-existing). Failure case throws an aggregated error listing every candidate's probe result plus a Windows-specific install hint pointing at python.org with the "Add Python to PATH" reminder. 6 new unit tests in `test/commands/init-internals.test.ts`.
notes: Patch on top of 0.5.6. Run `trellis update` (no `--migrate` needed). Codex users get hardened sub-agent role files (collab tools structurally disabled + the previously-dogfood-only "Load Trellis Context First" section). Windows users who previously had to install 0.4.0 first can now run `trellis init` directly on 0.5.7+. No script / hook / configurator behavior changed for non-Codex non-Windows users.
